title: The Columbine
content:
Gray lonely rocks about thee stand,
    Ignored of sun and dew,
    Yet is thy breath upon the land,
    To thy vocation true.

    So come they character to me
    That works in sunless ways,
    And I shall learn to give with thee
    Dark hills a constant praise.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Michael Earls, titled "The Columbine", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
